text: This chapter explores the intersection of peace studies and educational practice, addressing the systemic challenges and ethical imperatives involved in cultivating a culture of nonviolence within modern learning environments. By situating educational reform within the broader framework of social justice, the author examines how curricula can be reimagined to move beyond mere conflict resolution and toward the active dismantling of structural inequality. Drawing on diverse theoretical perspectives, the text critiques existing institutional barriers and proposes transformative pedagogical strategies designed to empower students as critical thinkers and global citizens. Ultimately, this work serves as a call to action for educators and policymakers to prioritize peace education as a fundamental component of democracy and human rights.
